subject: physics
title: 22M.1.SL.TZ2.13
question_html:
System X is at a temperature of 40 °C. Thermal energy is provided to system X until it reaches a temperature of 50 °C. System Y is at a temperature of 283 K. Thermal energy is provided to system Y until it reaches a temperature of 293 K.

What is the difference in the thermal energy provided to both systems?

A.  Zero

B.  Larger for X

C.  Larger for Y

D.  Cannot be determined with the data given
examiners_html: This question gives good discrimination although slightly more candidates chose option A instead of the correct option D. It is unusual that the correct response is 'cannot be determined' but the lack of mass or specific heat capacity in the data should have alerted candidates that they were not able to work out or compare how much thermal energy was supplied.
